clusters.title: Indu Dharmasena returns with riotous comedy “Love, Lies and Wildlife”

versions.title: Indu Dharmasena returns with riotous comedy “Love, Lies and Wildlife”
versions.deck: Indu Dharmasena’s comedy ‘Love, Lies and Wildlife’ takes the stage in a fast-paced farce where secrets unravel and nothing goes according to plan, at the Lionel Wendt Theatre on 8-10 May at 7:30 p.m.

clusters.title: Scope Cinemas hosts exclusive red-carpet premiere of Michael at Havelock City Mall

versions.title: Scope Cinemas hosts exclusive red-carpet premiere of Michael at Havelock City Mall
versions.deck: Scope Cinemas Scope Cinemas in collaboration with Universal Studios, Lionsgate and Liberty Cinemas, hosted an exclusive red-carpet premiere of Michael on 22 April 2026 at the Main Atrium of Havelock City Mall, followed by a special IMAX screening at the Scope Cinemas Multiplex.

clusters.title: Zonta Club III Colombo, Infinite Grace Foundation and Evolution Auto unite for special cause

versions.title: Zonta Club III Colombo, Infinite Grace Foundation and Evolution Auto unite for special cause
versions.deck: Zonta Club III of Colombo, in association with the Infinite Grace Foundation, powered by Evolution Auto presents a special screening of one the most awaited sequels of this season

clusters.title: Dhee launches “Vari Vari,” marking Sri Lanka as key milestone in global rise

versions.title: Dhee launches “Vari Vari,” marking Sri Lanka as key milestone in global rise
versions.deck: Acclaimed Australian-Tamil singer, songwriter and performer Dhee continues her rapid ascent on the global stage with the release of her latest single, “Vari Vari,” officially unveiled yesterday in Colombo.

clusters.title: Martin Wickramasinghe Trust lecture series concludes

versions.title: Martin Wickramasinghe Trust lecture series concludes
versions.deck: The Martin Wickramasinghe Trust concluded its long-running lecture series with a presentation at Sapumal Foundation in Colombo on 31 March 2026. Titled “Ways of Looking” and conducted by Uditha Devapriya, the lecture probed into Wickramasinghe’s views on art, culture, and art history.

clusters.title: Joint Opposition Alliance seeks probe into diesel procurement deal, alleges irregularities and losses

versions.title: Joint Opposition Alliance seeks probe into diesel procurement deal, alleges irregularities and losses
versions.deck: The Joint Opposition Alliance, a coalition representing several key political parties has formally lodged a complaint with the Commission to Investigate Allegations of Bribery or Corruption (CIABOC), calling for an investigation into an alleged irregular diesel procurement process involving the Ceylon Petroleum Corporation (CPC).

clusters.title: Cyber-crimes and management weaknesses

versions.title: Cyber-crimes and management weaknesses
versions.deck: Information and communication technologies (ICTs) are the cornerstone for sustainable development, but if they are not appropriately managed, they will impede progress towards the United Nations Global Sustainable Development Goals.

clusters.title: Eran outlines plans for resuscitating Sri Lanka Cricket

versions.title: Eran outlines plans for resuscitating Sri Lanka Cricket
versions.deck: Eran Wickramaratne in his inaugural address after assuming duties as Chairman of Sri Lanka Cricket’s (SLC) Transformation Committee yesterday said that there were two fundamental pillars which needed to be addressed to restore the pride and performance of the game – improved governance and excellence on the field.

clusters.title: Further shakeups to alter course of the League

versions.title: Further shakeups to alter course of the League
versions.deck: We have already seen clear signs from many teams who have taken Trinity Rugby as their role model to improving the quality of ball handling, containing their unforced errors to a jaw dropping couple, while a few others have followed Royal’s renewed path towards the heavens in crunching their sinfulness.

clusters.title: Omalka and Kavindu added to XV squad

versions.title: Omalka and Kavindu added to XV squad
versions.deck: Sri Lanka XV will take on the New Zealand Under 85 kg rugby outfit in the second tour match at the Nittawela Rugby Stadium in Kandy on 3 May, with kick-off scheduled for 4 p.m., played for the second edition of the Sir Graham Henry Trophy.

clusters.title: Harshitha, Kavisha clinch WT20I series for Sri Lanka

versions.title: Harshitha, Kavisha clinch WT20I series for Sri Lanka
versions.deck: Harshitha Samarawickrama blasted an unbeaten 49 off just 29 deliveries (4 fours, 2 sixes) to set up Sri Lanka’s series-clinching win over Bangladesh in the second WT20I played at Sylhet yesterday.

clusters.title: Colombo Kickerz Girls’ Under-14 off to Malaysia

versions.title: Colombo Kickerz Girls’ Under-14 off to Malaysia
versions.deck: Colombo Kickerz Football Academy is set to create history as their Girls Under-14 team left to Malaysia in order to compete in the prestigious KL Cup 2026, marking the academy’s first-ever all-girls international football campaign.

clusters.title: Daily FT to cost Rs. 100 from 4 May

versions.title: Daily FT to cost Rs. 100 from 4 May
versions.deck: Due to the increasing cost of distribution, energy, and imported materials required to publish the Daily FT, we regret to inform readers that we are compelled to increase the price of the Daily FT to Rs. 100 with effect from 4 May.

clusters.title: May Day mirror 2026: Sajith vs. Anura; SJB vs. JVP-NPP

versions.title: May Day mirror 2026: Sajith vs. Anura; SJB vs. JVP-NPP
versions.deck: May Day 2026 holds up a mirror before the country’s politico-electoral reality. Contrary to mirages of multipolarity, the political contest reveals itself as strategically bi-polar: Sajith Premadasa vs. Anura Dissanayake; SJB vs. JVP-NPP. The country’s essential choice of leadership in 2029 is discernible. It is a pity that the SLPP isn’t celebrating May Day. While 1 May is indeed a Poya (full moon) Day, it isn’t Vesak. clusters.title: Eran heads nine-member SLC Restructuring Committee

versions.title: Eran heads nine-member SLC Restructuring Committee
versions.deck: Sports Minister Sunil Kumara Gamage has appointed a nine-member Restructuring Committee for Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) to run the affairs of cricket in the country, with former MP and State Minister Eran Wickramaratne as Chairman.

clusters.title: Eran quits SJB committees

versions.title: Eran quits SJB committees
versions.deck: Former Samagi Jana Balawegaya (SJB) MP Eran Wickramaratne has resigned from the party’s Working Committee and Management Committee after assuming duties as Chairman of the Restructuring Committee of Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC), SJB General Secretary Ranjith Madduma Bandara said yesterday. SJB sources said that Wickramaratne was due to be suspended from the party for breach.

clusters.title: Seylan Bank 1Q PAT up 5.25% to Rs. 2.91 b

versions.title: Seylan Bank 1Q PAT up 5.25% to Rs. 2.91 b
versions.deck: Seylan Bank PLC has reported a Profit After Tax (PAT) of Rs. 2.91 billion in the first quarter of 2026, up 5.25% from a year ago. The bank said it recorded a Profit Before Income Tax (PBT) of Rs. 4,548 million in 1Q 2026, against Rs. 4,199 million a year ago, reflecting a growth of 8.31%.

clusters.title: JXG up 37% on debut

versions.title: JXG up 37% on debut
versions.deck: Janashakthi Ltd., (JXG) ended 37% on the up yesterday, debuting on the Colombo Stock Exchange (CSE) Main Board. The share price gained Rs. 3.70 to close at Rs. 13.70. JXG successfully concluded its Rs. 5 billion initial public offering (IPO) at Rs. 10 per share earlier this month. It was the largest IPO in the past 14 years.

clusters.title: Why do people really pay taxes?

versions.title: Why do people really pay taxes?
versions.deck: In Sri Lanka, as in many developing economies, most citizens pay taxes almost without noticing it. Every supermarket bill, litre of fuel, mobile reload, restaurant payment, or transport fare quietly includes a tax component. These indirect taxes are built into prices, collected instantly, and rarely questioned. Direct taxes, however, are different. Income tax is visible, personal, and intentional. clusters.title: Women in Sri Lanka’s Boards: The glass ceiling persists

versions.title: Women in Sri Lanka’s Boards: The glass ceiling persists
versions.deck: Sri Lanka presents a striking paradox. Women consistently outperform men in higher education, with tertiary enrolment at 23% for women against 16% for men, yet their participation in the formal labour force hovers around 34%, and their presence at the top of corporate Sri Lanka remains deeply marginal. The numbers are stark. An analysis of the LMD 100 for 2024/25 found that women hold just 140 of 945 directorships across 100 listed companies, a

versions.title: Shammi Silva & Co. steps down from SLC
versions.deck: So it’s finally official. Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) President Shammi Silva has, along with his members of the Executive Committee, tendered their resignations from their respective posts with effect from yesterday. An SLC media release states: “The President of SLC, Shammi Silva, has tendered his resignation from the post, effective today. clusters.title: FIFA confirms new World Cup rule on yellow cards to reduce suspensions

versions.title: FIFA confirms new World Cup rule on yellow cards to reduce suspensions
versions.deck: FIFA has tweaked World Cup rules on yellow cards to ensure fewer players are suspended for key elimination games, with single yellow cards to be cancelled after the group stage and after the quarter-finals. An extra amnesty for yellow cards – wiping player disciplinary records twice during the expanded tournament in North America – was proposed at a meeting on Tuesday of FIFA’s ruling council. Later, FIFA issued a statement approving the change

clusters.title: First-ever Colombo Marathon in September

versions.title: First-ever Colombo Marathon in September
versions.deck: The Sports and Youth Affairs Ministry this week announced that the ‘Colombo Marathon,’ organised for the first time by the Ministry, will be held in Colombo in September. Gamage emphasised that this event will be instrumental in placing Sri Lanka in a prominent position on the global map as a premier international sports tourism destination. clusters.title: SLC grants Rs. 10 m to rising javelin athlete Rumesh Tharanga

versions.title: SLC grants Rs. 10 m to rising javelin athlete Rumesh Tharanga
versions.deck: Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) provided a financial grant of Rs. 10 million to rising javelin thrower Rumesh Tharanga Pathirage, earlier this week. This support was extended under the direction of SLC President Shammi Silva and the Executive Committee of SLC following a request made by Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Sunil Kumara Gamage. versions.title: Scara Live powers South Asia’s first-ever Tapeball Asia Cup in Sri Lanka
versions.deck: Sri Lanka takes centre stage and is set to make history as Scara Live, the live experiences vertical of global sports, gaming, and culture agency Scara, has partnered Afghanistan Tapeball Cricket to launch for the first time the ‘Tapeball Asia Cup’ and first official continental Tapeball tournament sanctioned by the International Tapeball Council. versions.deck: Dear Minister Paulraj, We hope you remember us. We came to meet you on 28 February 2025 with the Child Protection Alliance to talk about ending corporal punishment in schools in Sri Lanka. This was the photo we took on that day. clusters.title: Revenue leakages at SriLankan Airlines

versions.title: Revenue leakages at SriLankan Airlines
versions.deck: Internet bookings were introduced in 1995 by British Airways in Colombo mainly to bypass third parties, i.e. agents, sub-agents and other commission-sharing partners. A customer pays for a ticket online in his hotel room, and the airline gets net revenue without payouts. clusters.title: Treasury Secretary declines CoPF summons today

versions.title: Treasury Secretary declines CoPF summons today
versions.deck: Treasury Secretary Dr. Harshana Suriyapperuma has declined to appear before the Parliamentary Committee on Public Finance (CoPF) today stating that President and Finance Minister Anura Kumara Dissanayake was expected to address Parliament on the $ 2.5 million Treasury breach. clusters.title: The big risks of being a Bank Non-Executive Chair or Director

versions.title: The big risks of being a Bank Non-Executive Chair or Director
versions.deck: By a Banker There is a quiet myth in corporate Sri Lanka that serving on a bank Board is a mark of distinction — a final badge of honour for a successful career. For too many, it is treated as prestige, networking, a comfort role, or a retirement supplement wrapped in titles, Board fees, and ceremonial influence. That mindset is precisely where the danger begins. Banking is not another corporate sector. It is not manufacturing, retail, or consu

clusters.title: The second silent bottleneck

versions.title: The second silent bottleneck
versions.deck: In the 1980s, IBM recruited graduates from any discipline. My intake included graduates in entomology, neuroscience, mathematics, theology, law (me), and linguistics. What we had in common was not a coding language or STEM training. It was a high level of linguistic and analytical literacy. clusters.title: The peace walk and the dog saint

versions.title: The peace walk and the dog saint
versions.deck: The woman venerates the dog, or does she? This was the minor controversy unfolding at the beginning of the ‘Walk for Peace’ that had reached Sri Lanka, following its remarkably successful conclusion in the USA (even though the whole exercise seems to have had little direct and immediate effect on the imperial war machinery there). clusters.title: Cabinet keeps $ 2.5 m Treasury breach off agenda

versions.title: Cabinet keeps $ 2.5 m Treasury breach off agenda
versions.deck: The Government said yesterday that neither President and Finance Minister Anura Kumara Dissanayake nor the Cabinet had discussed the reported $ 2.5 million Treasury cyber breach and internal investigation, leaving the matter to the Finance Ministry and external investigative agencies.
